howToPage.description: Step-by-step guides for Bonfire features and event actions.
howToPage.heading: Guides for getting things done
howToPage.subtitle: Clear practical walkthroughs for Bonfire members and event visitors.

checkIn.title: Event check-in
checkIn.description: Learn the full check-in flow from login to getting scanned by crew.

engagementSignals.title: Sparks and boosts
engagementSignals.description: Understand engagement signals, limits, and how visibility scoring works.

eventRsvp.title: Event RSVP
eventRsvp.description: How RSVP works, where to find events, waitlist behavior, and calendar export.

registration.title: Registration and sign-in
registration.description: Understand email codes, passkeys, and why first sign-in creates your account.

profilePrivacy.title: Profile privacy
profilePrivacy.description: Understand public profile visibility, check-in visibility, and privacy controls.

speaking.title: Speaking at Bonfire
speaking.description: How to apply as a speaker, what makes a strong proposal, and what happens next.

accountDeletion.title: Account deletion
accountDeletion.description: Understand the intent/confirm flow, one-time code, and permanent deletion warning.

checkInHowTo.title: How to check in at an event | {brandName}
checkInHowTo.description: A quick step-by-step manual for event check-in.
checkInHowTo.heading: How to check in at an event
checkInHowTo.subtitle: Follow these steps when you arrive. It takes less than a minute.
checkInHowTo.heroLead: Every check-in helps build a stronger, more connected Bonfire community.
checkInHowTo.heroSublead: Small action, real signal. You show up, we grow together.

one.title: Login or create account
one.description: Sign in first. If you are new, create your account and continue.

two.title: Open account page
two.description: Go to your account page where your check-in tools are available.

three.title: Click Check-in
three.description: Open your check-in pass to display your personal QR code.

four.title: Ask crew to scan it
four.description: Show the QR code to crew at the event entrance and let them scan it.

five.title: Be part of community
five.description: Your check-ins are shown on your profile when it is public.

rally.title: Why it matters
rally.description: When people check in, we can better understand attendance, improve planning, and celebrate the people who consistently show up for each other.

one.title: Show up
one.description: Your presence is visible and appreciated.

two.title: Support events
two.description: Check-ins help us plan better future meetups.

three.title: Inspire others
three.description: Consistent participation motivates the whole community.

benefits.title: About benefits right now
benefits.description: We are still deciding what exact benefits check-ins will unlock in the future. Checking in now already helps the community and event planning.

cta.title: Ready for your next check-in?
cta.description: Start now and make your next event count.

registrationHowTo.title: Registration and sign-in guide | {brandName}
registrationHowTo.description: How Bonfire registration works with email codes and passkeys.
registrationHowTo.heading: Registration and sign-in
registrationHowTo.subtitle: No separate signup form. Your first successful sign-in creates your account.
registrationHowTo.heroSublead: You verify ownership of your email, then your account is created automatically.

items.one: Open login and enter your email.
items.two: Complete the human check and request a one-time code.
items.three: Check your inbox and enter the code on the same login screen.
items.four: Codes are short-lived and one-time; if it expires, request a new one.

items.one: There is no separate registration page.
items.two: After a valid email code is confirmed, your account is created (or reused if it already exists).
items.three: Then you are signed in and redirected to your account page.

items.one: First, sign in with email code at least once.
items.two: Open Account and add a passkey in Security.
items.three: Next time, use the passkey button on login for faster sign-in on supported devices.

safety.title: Why passkeys are safer
safety.description: Passkeys reduce common account-takeover risks compared to one-time codes and passwords.

items.one: No shared secret to type, so phishing pages cannot steal your password.
items.two: Cryptographic login is bound to your device and verified against the real domain.
items.three: Biometrics or device PIN add local user verification before sign-in.

notes.title: Good to know
notes.description: If login fails, request a fresh code. You can always continue with email code even if passkey is unavailable on a device.

profilePrivacyHowTo.title: Profile privacy guide | {brandName}
profilePrivacyHowTo.description: How profile privacy works, including check-in visibility and account controls.
profilePrivacyHowTo.heading: Profile privacy guide
profilePrivacyHowTo.subtitle: Clear visibility rules so you can control what others see.
profilePrivacyHowTo.heroLead: You control if your profile is discoverable to others.
profilePrivacyHowTo.heroSublead: Check-ins and engagement visibility depend on your profile privacy state.

items.one: Public profile means your profile can be visited and shared.
items.two: Private profile reduces what others can discover from your account.
items.three: You can review your current privacy state directly in Account.

items.one: Check-ins are displayed on profile only when your profile is public.
items.two: If profile is private, check-in history is not publicly listed.
items.three: Changing profile visibility updates what visitors can see.

items.one: Open Account settings to review profile and security options.
items.two: Use passkeys for stronger sign-in security when available.
items.three: If unsure, start private and move to public when ready.

notes.title: Good to know
notes.description: Public visibility helps community discovery. Private visibility gives stricter control over your account presence.

speakingHowTo.title: Speaking at Bonfire guide | {brandName}
speakingHowTo.description: How to submit a talk and what to expect from review and scheduling.
speakingHowTo.heading: Speaking at Bonfire
speakingHowTo.subtitle: A practical path from idea to stage.
speakingHowTo.heroLead: You do not need to be a professional speaker to apply.
speakingHowTo.heroSublead: Clear topic, concrete value, and honest story beats polish alone.

items.one: Open the Speak page and submit your topic proposal.
items.two: Describe audience value: what people will learn, solve, or rethink.
items.three: Add practical context so reviewers understand scope and level.

items.one: Prefer focused topics over broad themes.
items.two: Include real examples, tradeoffs, or lessons learned.
items.three: Keep title and abstract specific, short, and outcome-oriented.

items.one: Crew reviews submissions and may request clarifications.
items.two: Accepted talks are coordinated with format, timing, and logistics.
items.three: If not selected this round, you can refine and re-submit.

notes.title: Community-first mindset
notes.description: The best talks help others move forward. Practical depth and honesty consistently resonate with the community.

eventRsvpHowTo.title: Event RSVP guide | {brandName}
eventRsvpHowTo.description: How event RSVP works on Bonfire and partner platforms.
eventRsvpHowTo.heading: Event RSVP guide
eventRsvpHowTo.subtitle: Everything first-time visitors need to know before attending.
eventRsvpHowTo.heroLead: RSVP is simple and worth doing early.
eventRsvpHowTo.heroSublead: It helps us plan venue capacity, seating, and updates.

items.one: Open an event detail page from Upcoming Events.
items.two: Use the RSVP action and follow the selected registration platform.
items.three: After a successful RSVP, keep your confirmation for entry updates.

items.one: Upcoming Events page lists current and next meetups.
items.two: Homepage events section highlights key upcoming sessions.
items.three: Each event detail page includes registration options and schedule info.

items.one: If capacity is full, the registration platform may offer a waitlist.
items.two: Waitlist confirmation depends on seat releases and organizer updates.
items.three: Stay subscribed to the event notifications so you do not miss your slot.

items.one: On event pages, use Add to Calendar when available.
items.two: You can usually choose Google Calendar or download an .ics file.
items.three: Adding it to your calendar helps you keep reminders and location details.

accountDeletionHowTo.title: Account deletion guide | {brandName}
accountDeletionHowTo.description: How account deletion works, including code verification and permanence.
accountDeletionHowTo.heading: Account deletion guide
accountDeletionHowTo.subtitle: Transparent explanation of the deletion flow before you confirm.
accountDeletionHowTo.heroLead: Deletion is permanent and immediate after confirmation.
accountDeletionHowTo.heroSublead: Please review the steps carefully before proceeding.

items.one: Open Account and go to the danger zone.
items.two: Confirm intent to start deletion.
items.three: Request the one-time deletion code.
items.four: Enter the code and confirm deletion.

items.one: The deletion code is sent to your account email.
items.two: It is short-lived and can expire, so use it promptly.
items.three: If needed, request a new code and retry the confirmation step.

items.one: Deletion cannot be undone.
items.two: Your account data and related activity are removed.
items.three: After deletion, you are redirected away from the account area.

engagementSignalsHowTo.title: Sparks and boosts guide | {brandName}
engagementSignalsHowTo.description: How engagement signals affect visibility and what limits apply.
engagementSignalsHowTo.heading: Sparks and boosts guide
engagementSignalsHowTo.subtitle: Understand what these signals mean and how to use them well.
engagementSignalsHowTo.heroLead: Sparks are lightweight feedback, boosts are stronger votes.
engagementSignalsHowTo.heroSublead: Together they shape discovery, especially in trending views.

sparks.title: Sparks
sparks.summary: Fast, lightweight reactions that help talks gain early momentum.

items.one: Sparks are lightweight likes and easier to give.
items.two: They are treated as a softer signal than boosts.
items.three: Sparks still help recordings gain momentum.

sparks.impactBody: Sparks increase trending score and can help recordings surface, especially when engagement is recent.

boosts.title: Boosts

items.one: Boosts come from signed-in accounts.
items.two: Each account can boost a recording once.
items.three: Boosts carry stronger weight in visibility ranking.

boosts.impactBody: A few boosts can noticeably increase visibility because boosts are weighted more strongly than sparks.

items.one: Sparks are easier to create and best for quick appreciation.
items.two: Boosts are scarcer and indicate stronger intent.
items.three: Best results usually come from a healthy mix of both signals.

limits.title: Limits and allocation

items.one: Boosts are limited monthly.
items.two: Unused boosts can accumulate only up to the configured cap.
items.three: Plan boosts for recordings you want to highlight most.

limits.callout: Because boosts are limited, use them where you want to send a strong, deliberate signal.

items.one: Recent engagement generally has stronger effect than old activity.
items.two: A mix of sparks and boosts can improve discoverability.
items.three: Consistent community engagement helps quality talks surface.
